config.description: Dissertation D07 as a coordinated T2 launcher with one local live sensor, one AP-B UDP helper node, and one Node-C 2.4 GHz AP plus ESP32-S3 third node

sensor.label: node-a
sensor.public_command_role: primary AP UDP summary capture command

auxiliary_sensors.label: node-c
auxiliary_sensors.public_command_role: Node-C AP UDP summary capture command

capture_blocks.objective: confirm that all three node streams are active before capture
capture_blocks.scenario: three-node warm-up and subset sanity check
static presence in the shared interaction zone

capture_blocks.objective: collect the first moving three-node segment
capture_blocks.scenario: slow motion through the shared interaction zone

capture_blocks.objective: capture the reverse transition to empty
capture_blocks.scenario: empty recovery after the first cycle

capture_blocks.objective: provide an in-session repeat for the subset ranking surface
provide an in-session repeat for the moving subset comparison
complete the three-node bundle and subset manifest

honesty_notes: D07 exercises the three-node subset-selection launcher surface.
Node B must use the same real AP-B UDP summary path that made D05-D06 claim-grade; the RPi4 UART path is diagnostic only unless it later gets a separate clean transport proof.

The third node is the dedicated `RPi5 + ESP32-S3` path where Node C hosts `<redacted-node-c-ssid>` on channel 6 and the ESP32-S3 joins that 2.4 GHz AP; if Node C falls back to a stub or direct-attach geometry prevents a credible sensing path, keep the result below claim-grade evidence.

Confirm from the captured Node-C stream that the `ESP32-S3` emits `CSI_PROFILE fw_profile=rpi5_node_c_24g fw_role=live`; if it still reports `rpi5_node_c_test_24g`, the deprecated `rpi5_mgmt_24g` alias, or any other test image, treat Node C as not yet live for `T2`.
The subset manifest must be completed after the run before any ranking claim is made.

operator_notes: D07 tests heterogeneity and subset value across three nodes on one shared timeline.

Use the corrected D07-D09 clock-face layout: Node A ESP32-C5 connected to the laptop by USB at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees; primary router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees; Node B RPi5 at 9 o'clock / 270 degrees; Node B RPi4 with USB-connected ESP32-C5 at 3 o'clock / 90 degrees; Node C RPi5 at 7:30 / 7.5 o'clock / 225 degrees; Node C ESP32-S3 power-only receiver off the 1.00 m ring at 1:30 / 1.5 o'clock / 45 degrees, 2.00 m from the experiment center.
Clock-to-degree convention: 12 o'clock is 0 degrees, 3 o'clock is 90 degrees, 6 o'clock is 180 degrees, and 9 o'clock is 270 degrees, measured clockwise from 12.

Primary ring radius: Node A ESP32-C5, router/AP, Node B RPi5, Node B RPi4 plus ESP32-C5, and Node C RPi5 are each 1.00 m from the experiment center; the Node C ESP32-S3 is the only off-ring device and sits in the separate 1:30 corner at 2.00 m from the center.
Controlled primary sensing diameter: Node A ESP32-C5 at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees to the router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees, for a 2.00 m line through the experiment center.

Router/AP, active esp32-c5-a, rpi4-a, rpi5-a, Node-C RPi5, and Node-C ESP32-S3 stay at 0.50 m height unless the operator records a deliberate offset; the capture laptop stays adjacent to the Node A USB setup and must not intrude into another sensing corridor.

Node A is live on the primary sensing plane, Node B uses the AP-B UDP summary plane, and Node C uses the dedicated `RPi5 + ESP32-S3` 2.4 GHz AP path at `<node-c-ap-address>` with ESP32-S3 summaries captured over UDP.

Node A is captured over the primary-router UDP DNAT listener on the laptop; `<local-serial-device>` is used for liveness/reset and serial drain, not as the claim-grade data transport for the current firmware image.
Node C physical geometry must be recorded as an RPi5 2.4 GHz AP/router and a separately placed ESP32-S3 receiver; USB is only a local flashing/preflight transport, not the claim-grade capture path.
Do not use RPi4 UART as the D07 claim-grade node-B capture path unless it has a separate clean transport proof; the AP-B UDP summary path is the claim-grade node-B stream.
Geometry requirement: record the ESP32-S3 receiver position relative to the Node-C RPi5 AP before treating the Node-C path as separated sensing evidence.
Keep the interaction zone centered on the same room midpoint and record any asymmetry immediately.

Post-run public status: captured Node-C stream reported `fw_profile=rpi5_node_c_24g` and `fw_role=live`; this public metadata preserves the claim-grade D07 verdict without retaining private addresses or adapter names.
topology_notes: D07-D09 clock/degree convention: 12 o'clock = 0 degrees, 3 o'clock = 90 degrees, 6 o'clock = 180 degrees, 9 o'clock = 270 degrees, measured clockwise from 12.

Primary 1.00 m ring around the experiment center: Node A ESP32-C5 connected to the laptop by USB at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees; primary router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees; Node B RPi5 at 9 o'clock / 270 degrees; Node B RPi4 with USB-connected ESP32-C5 at 3 o'clock / 90 degrees; Node C RPi5 at 7:30 / 7.5 o'clock / 225 degrees, midway along the perimeter between the router/AP and the Node B RPi5.
Off-ring Node C receiver: ESP32-S3 is connected only to power in the separate 1:30 / 1.5 o'clock / 45 degrees corner, 2.00 m from the experiment center.
Controlled primary sensing diameter: Node A ESP32-C5 at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees to the router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees.
Vertical placement: router/AP, active esp32-c5-a, rpi5-a, rpi4-a, Node-C RPi5, and Node-C ESP32-S3 at 0.50 m unless otherwise recorded; capture laptop adjacent to the Node A USB setup.
Node A is the live primary stand; Node B uses the AP-B UDP summary path; Node C uses the dedicated Node-C 2.4 GHz AP and ESP32-S3 UDP summary path.
Node-C RPi5 AP/router placement is 7:30 / 225 degrees on the 1.00 m ring; the ESP32-S3 receiver was power-only at 1:30 / 45 degrees, 2.00 m from the experiment center.
Record which subset combinations were considered credible for analysis and whether any helper gear intruded into the 12-to-6 sensing corridor.

checkpoints.description: controller-side mid-session alignment checkpoint before empty recovery

ring_devices.device: Node A ESP32-C5 connected to laptop by USB
Node B RPi4 with USB-connected ESP32-C5

off_ring_devices.device: Node C ESP32-S3 connected only to power

physical_topology.primary_sensing_diameter: Node A ESP32-C5 at 12 oclock / 0 degrees to primary AP at 6 oclock / 180 degrees

sanitization_summary: removed absolute local paths, private host addresses, AP credentials, adapter names, and runtime-specific environment details
kept all three raw capture logs and the operator/timing event logs for auditability
added explicit clock-face and degree topology for the D07-D09 physical setup
